question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply account.id=eq:0.0.1000&account.id=gte:0.0.1000 to trigger the verifyEqualOrRange() check ('Can't support both range and equal for account.id'), and use the error response to confirm that the EQ operator is mutually exclusive with range operators, enabling systematic probing of the validation logic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowanceRequest.getBounds() logic where primaryBound is ownerOrSpenderIds if non-empty, otherwise tokenIds, by supplying only token.id without account.id to trigger the 'token.id parameter must have account.id present' error, using this as a boolean oracle to confirm whether the ownerOrSpenderIds bound is empty in the service layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with token.id=lt:0.0.5000 without any account.id parameter to trigger the 'Requires the presence of an lte or eq account.id parameter' error in checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity, confirming that the upper-bound token.id constraint requires a corresponding account.id upper bound, leaking query planning logic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LinkFactoryImpl.isEmptyRange() method's string-based numeric parsing (Long.parseLong on query parameter values) by injecting a crafted pagination next-link with a manipulated account.id range that bypasses the empty-range check, causing the server to execute a query with an unintended range and return allowances outside the original query scope?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user manually craft a next-link URL (as returned in NftAllowancesResponse.links.next) with modified account.id and token.id parameters to bypass the pagination cursor and jump to an arbitrary position in the allowance dataset, accessing allowance records that would not normally be returned in sequential pagination?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the addParamMapToQueryParams() logic in LinkFactoryImpl, which preserves original query parameters in the next link, by injecting additional query parameters in the initial request that get carried forward in pagination links, potentially poisoning subsequent requests with attacker-controlled filter values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard/realm combination different from the default (e.g., 1.0.1000 instead of 0.0.1000) in the account.id range parameter to query allowances across non-default shards, potentially accessing allowance data that is not intended to be exposed through the default shard's API endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the addExtractedParamsToQueryParams() logic in LinkFactoryImpl where the exclusive boolean is determined by whether the next sort field has an EQ value, by crafting initial query parameters that manipulate the sortEqMap, causing the pagination link to use GT instead of GTE (or vice versa), skipping one allowance record per page and creating gaps in the enumeration?
